198	            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
199	            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
200	            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]
201	
202	            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
203	            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
204	                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
205	            else:
206	                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
207	                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
208	                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
209	                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
210	        pass
211	
212	        importlib.reload(bnb)
213	        importlib.reload(triton)
214	        try:
215	            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
216	            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
217	                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
218	                except: pass
219	            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
220	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
221	            libcuda_dirs()
222	        except:
223	            warnings.warn(
224	                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
225	                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
226	                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
227	                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
228	                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
229	                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
230	            )
231	    pass
232	elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
233	    # NO-OP for rocm device
234	    pass
235	elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
236	    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future

120	def _fast_prepare_inputs_for_generation(self, input_ids, attention_mask=None, **kwargs,):
121	    past_key_values = kwargs.get("past_key_values", None)
122	    if past_key_values is not None:
123	        # Check for uninitialized DynamicCache
124	        if len(past_key_values) == 0:
125	            past_key_values = None
126	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
127	        # New since 4.56
128	        elif hasattr(past_key_values, "get_seq_length") and past_key_values.get_seq_length() == 0:
129	            past_key_values = None
130	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
131	        else:
132	            bs, cache_length = input_ids.shape
133	            input_ids = input_ids[:,[-1]]
134	
135	            # Get to the base model
136	            base_model = self
137	            if hasattr(base_model, 'base_model_prefix'):
138	                base_model = getattr(base_model, base_model.base_model_prefix)
139	
140	            if hasattr(base_model, "_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position"):
141	                def needs_device_kw(fn) -> bool:
142	                    try:
143	                        sig = inspect.signature(inspect.unwrap(fn))
144	                        return "device" in sig.parameters
145	                    except:
146	                        # transformers <= 4.51.3 includes device arg but > 4.51.3 does not
147	                        return transformers_version < Version("4.52.0")
148	
149	                kwargs = {
150	                    "sequence_length": 1,
151	                    "target_length": cache_length,
152	                    "dtype": self.dtype,
153	                    "cache_position": torch.arange(cache_length, cache_length+1, device=input_ids.device),
154	                    "batch_size": bs,
155	                    "config": self.config,
156	                    "past_key_values": past_key_values,
157	                }
158	                try:
159	                    if needs_device_kw(base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position):
160	                        kwargs["device"] = input_ids.device
161	                except:
162	                    print(f"Unsloth: Could not inspect signature of {base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position}")
163	
164	                attention_mask = base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position(
165	                    attention_mask,
166	                    **kwargs,
167	                )
168	            else:
169	                attention_mask = attention_mask[:,[-1]]
170	                if transformers_version <= Version("4.52.4"):
171	                    logger.warning_once(
172	                        f"{self.__class__.__name__} has no `_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position` method "
173	                        "defined in its base modeling class. Compiled forward passes will be sub-optimal. If you're "
174	                        "writing code, see Llama for an example implementation. If you're a user, please report this "
175	                        "issue on GitHub."
176	                    )
177	
178	    if "cache_position" in kwargs:
179	        kwargs["position_ids"] = kwargs["cache_position"]
180	    return { "input_ids" : input_ids, "attention_mask": attention_mask, **kwargs, }
181	pass

216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,

219	
220	    # Push to hub

222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,

227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass

1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",

1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass
137	pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]

2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:
2601	        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
2602	        if commit_message is not None:
2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:

2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag
2621	    if hasattr(self, "config"):

2634	    pass
2635

2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641

## Comprehensive Unsloth Project Structure Analysis

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the unsloth project. Here's what I found:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/
├── .github/
├── .gitignore
├── CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
├── CONTRIBUTING.md
├── LICENSE
├── README.md
├── images/
├── pyproject.toml
├── unsloth/                    # Main library
├── unsloth-cli.py              # CLI interface
└── tests/                      # Test suite (35 Python test files)
```

### 2. MAIN UNSLOTH DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                          # Main public API (imports from models, save, chat_templates, etc.)
├── _auto_install.py
├── chat_templates.py                    # Chat template handling
├── import_fixes.py                      # Import patches for dependencies
├── save.py                              # Core saving/export functionality (105KB)
├── tokenizer_utils.py                   # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                           # Training utilities and patching
├── dataprep/                            # Data preparation
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── synthetic.py
│   └── synthetic_configs.py
├── kernels/                             # CUDA/Triton kernels
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py
│   ├── fast_lora.py                     # LoRA kernel implementations
│   ├── flex_attention.py
│   ├── geglu.py
│   ├── layernorm.py
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py
│   ├── rope_embedding.py
│   ├── swiglu.py
│   ├── utils.py
│   └── moe/                             # Mixture of Experts kernels
├── models/                              # Model-specific implementations (22 files)
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── _utils.py
│   ├── llama.py                         # Base FastLlamaModel (131KB)
│   ├── loader.py                        # FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastModel
│   ├── mistral.py
│   ├── qwen2.py
│   ├── qwen3.py
│   ├── qwen3_moe.py
│   ├── gemma.py
│   ├── gemma2.py
│   ├── granite.py
│   ├── falcon_h1.py
│   ├── cohere.py
│   ├── dpo.py                           # DPO trainer patching
│   ├── rl.py                            # RL/reinforcement learning
│   ├── rl_replacements.py
│   ├── vision.py                        # Vision model support
│   ├── mapper.py                        # Model mapping utilities
│   └── loader_utils.py
├── registry/                            # Model registry
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── registry.py
│   ├── REGISTRY.md
│   ├── _llama.py
│   ├── _mistral.py
│   ├── _qwen.py
│   ├── _gemma.py
│   ├── _phi.py
│   └── _deepseek.py
└── utils/                               # Utility modules
```

### 3. SAVE AND EXPORT METHODS

Found in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/save.py` (2,679+ lines):

**Main Public Methods (patched onto models via `patch_saving_functions`):**
- `model.save_pretrained_merged()` → line 2413 (generic) / 1321 (llama-specific)
- `model.push_to_hub_merged()` → line 2456 (generic) / 1364 (llama-specific)
- `model.save_pretrained_gguf()` → line 1768
- `model.push_to_hub_gguf()` → line 1951
- `model.save_pretrained_ggml()` → line 2211 (LoRA-specific GGML format)
- `model.push_to_hub_ggml()` → line 2143
- `model.save_pretrained_torchao()` → line 2502 (TorchAO quantization)
- `model.push_to_hub()` (patched) → dynamically generated at line 2586

**Core Functions:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` → line 205 (main save orchestrator)
- `save_to_gguf()` → line 952 (GGUF conversion with llama.cpp)
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` → line 2276 (generic vision/non-Llama GGUF)
- `unsloth_save_lora_to_custom_dir()` → line 2129
- `unsloth_generic_save()` → line 2356 (handles all model types)
- `patch_saving_functions()` → line 2569 (applies methods to model instances)

**Helper Functions:**
- `install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()` → line 763
- `install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()` → line 770
- `save_to_gguf_blocking()` → line 905
- `create_huggingface_repo()` → line 1456
- `upload_to_huggingface()` → line 1495
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` → line 1618
- `push_to_ollama()` → line 1735

### 4. MAIN __init__.py FILES - PUBLIC API

**`/unsloth/__init__.py` (line 261-269):**
```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

**Exported from `save.py`:**
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model`
- `save_to_gguf`
- `patch_saving_functions`
- `create_huggingface_repo`

**Exported from `models/__init__.py`:**
- `FastLlamaModel` (base class for all language models)
- `FastLanguageModel` (static `.from_pretrained()` loader)
- `FastVisionModel` (vision model loader)
- `FastTextModel` (text model wrapper)
- `FastModel` (general model class)
- Model implementations: `FastMistralModel`, `FastQwen2Model`, `FastQwen3Model`, `FastGemmaModel`, `FastCohereModel`, `FastGraniteModel`, `FastFalconH1Model`, etc.
- `PatchDPOTrainer`, `PatchKTOTrainer`
- `PatchFastRL`, `vLLMSamplingParams`

### 5. ONNX-RELATED CODE

**Result: NO ONNX SUPPORT FOUND**

Searched pattern: `ONNX|onnx`
- No files returned
- No ONNX export functionality exists in the codebase
- The library focuses on: LoRA merging, GGUF (llama.cpp) conversion, GGML formats, and TorchAO quantization

### 6. MAIN MODEL CLASSES

All found in model files, inheriting from `FastLlamaModel` (line 1778 in llama.py):

**Base Classes:**
- `FastLlamaModel` (1778) - Base class, 1000+ line implementations
- `FastBaseModel` (vision.py:283) - Vision models base

**Language Model Classes:**
- `FastLanguageModel` (loader.py:107) - Main loader with `.from_pretrained()` static method
- `FastMistralModel` (mistral.py:394)
- `FastQwen2Model` (qwen2.py:38)
- `FastQwen3Model` (qwen3.py:387)
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` (qwen3_moe.py:158)
- `FastGemmaModel` (gemma.py:336)
- `FastGemma2Model` (gemma2.py:459)
- `FastGraniteModel` (granite.py:456)
- `FastCohereModel` (cohere.py:456)
- `FastFalconH1Model` (falcon_h1.py:648)

**Wrapper/Loader Classes:**
- `FastModel` (loader.py:526) - General model wrapper
- `FastVisionModel` (loader.py:995) - Vision model handler
- `FastTextModel` (loader.py:998) - Text model wrapper

**Supporting Classes (not model-specific):**
- `LlamaRotaryEmbedding` (llama.py:1363)
- `LlamaLinearScalingRotaryEmbedding` (llama.py:1445)
- `LlamaExtendedRotaryEmbedding` (llama.py:1479)
- `LongRopeRotaryEmbedding` (llama.py:1587)

**Registry Info Classes:**
- `LlamaModelInfo` (registry/_llama.py:8)
- `LlamaVisionModelInfo` (registry/_llama.py:15)
- `MistralSmallModelInfo` (registry/_mistral.py:11)
- `QwenModelInfo`, `QwenVLModelInfo`, `QwenQwQModelInfo`, `QwenQVQPreviewModelInfo` (registry/_qwen.py)
- `GemmaModelInfo` (registry/_gemma.py:6)
- `PhiModelInfo` (registry/_phi.py:6)
- `DeepseekV3ModelInfo`, `DeepseekR1ModelInfo` (registry/_deepseek.py)

### 7. SAVE METHODS ARCHITECTURE

**Save Method Types** (parameter in most functions):
1. `"lora"` - Save only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
2. `"merged_16bit"` - Merge LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF)
3. `"merged_4bit"` - Merge LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)
4. `"merged_4bit_forced"` - Force 4bit merge despite warnings

**Export Formats Supported:**
- GGUF (llama.cpp compatible) - via `save_to_gguf()`, `save_pretrained_gguf()`
- GGML (LoRA-specific) - via `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`
- TorchAO quantized - via `save_pretrained_torchao()`
- Standard HuggingFace format (merged or LoRA)
- Push to HuggingFace Hub support

**Key Features:**
- Automatic 4-bit to 16-bit dequantization during merge
- GGUF quantization options: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `f32`, etc.
- Automatic llama.cpp installation and compilation
- Memory-efficient saving with shard support
- HuggingFace Hub integration with automatic tagging
- Ollama model creation support

### 8. SUPPORTING INFRASTRUCTURE

**Kernels** (custom CUDA/Triton implementations):
- Fast LoRA implementations
- Cross-entropy loss
- RMS and layer norm
- RoPE embeddings
- SwigLU and GeLU variants
- Flex attention
- Mixture of Experts (MOE) kernels with grouped GEMM

**Trainer Integration:**
- `UnslothTrainer` (extends SFTTrainer)
- `UnslothTrainingArguments`
- Backwards-compatible TRL trainer patching
- Gradient accumulation fixes

**Test Suite** (35 test files in `/tests/`):
- Language model saving/merging tests
- Vision model tests
- QLora training tests
- Push to Hub tests
- Model perplexity validation
- Text-to-speech model tests
- Non-PEFT model tests

### 9. KEY FILES BY SIZE/IMPORTANCE

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `save.py` | 2,679 | All save/export/merge functionality |
| `llama.py` | 1,778 | Base model class & optimized forward passes |
| `models/_utils.py` | ~2,000 | Model patching & utility functions |
| `rl.py` | ~1,500 | RL training integration |
| `models/loader.py` | 995+ | Model loading & initialization |
| `chat_templates.py` | 129KB | Chat template registry |

### SUMMARY

The Unsloth project is a PyTorch library focused on **efficient LoRA fine-tuning** with comprehensive save/export capabilities. It provides:
- **4 main save methods**: LoRA-only, merged 16-bit, merged 4-bit, and TorchAO quantized
- **3 export formats**: Standard HF format, GGUF (for llama.cpp), and GGML
- **16 supported model architectures**: LLaMA, Mistral, Qwen (2, 3, 3-MoE), Gemma, Gemma2, Granite, Cohere, Falcon-H1, and vision models
- **Zero ONNX support** - focuses on open-source deployment formats (GGUF, GGML)
- **Dynamic method patching**: Adds save methods to model instances at runtime
- **Full HuggingFace Hub integration**: Push, tagging, and repository creation
- **Custom CUDA kernels**: Optimized for training speed and memory efficiency

- `DeepseekV3ModelInfo`, `DeepseekR1ModelInfo` (registry/_deepseek.py)

### 7. SAVE METHODS ARCHITECTURE

**Save Method Types** (parameter in most functions):
1. `"lora"` - Save only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
2. `"merged_16bit"` - Merge LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF)
3. `"merged_4bit"` - Merge LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)
4. `"merged_4bit_forced"` - Force 4bit merge despite warnings

**Export Formats Supported:**
- GGUF (llama.cpp compatible) - via `save_to_gguf()`, `save_pretrained_gguf()`
- GGML (LoRA-specific) - via `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`
- TorchAO quantized - via `save_pretrained_torchao()`
- Standard HuggingFace format (merged or LoRA)
- Push to HuggingFace Hub support

**Key Features:**
- Automatic 4-bit to 16-bit dequantization during merge
- GGUF quantization options: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `f32`, etc.
- Automatic llama.cpp installation and compilation
- Memory-efficient saving with shard support
- HuggingFace Hub integration with automatic tagging
- Ollama model creation support

### 8. SUPPORTING INFRASTRUCTURE

**Kernels** (custom CUDA/Triton implementations):
- Fast LoRA implementations
- Cross-entropy loss
- RMS and layer norm
- RoPE embeddings
- SwigLU and GeLU variants
- Flex attention
- Mixture of Experts (MOE) kernels with grouped GEMM

**Trainer Integration:**
- `UnslothTrainer` (extends SFTTrainer)
- `UnslothTrainingArguments`
- Backwards-compatible TRL trainer patching
- Gradient accumulation fixes

**Test Suite** (35 test files in `/tests/`):
- Language model saving/merging tests
- Vision model tests
- QLora training tests
- Push to Hub tests
- Model perplexity validation
- Text-to-speech model tests
- Non-PEFT model tests

### 9. KEY FILES BY SIZE/IMPORTANCE

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `save.py` | 2,679 | All save/export/merge functionality |
| `llama.py` | 1,778 | Base model class & optimized forward passes |
| `models/_utils.py` | ~2,000 | Model patching & utility functions |
| `rl.py` | ~1,500 | RL training integration |
| `models/loader.py` | 995+ | Model loading & initialization |
| `chat_templates.py` | 129KB | Chat template registry |

### SUMMARY

The Unsloth project is a PyTorch library focused on **efficient LoRA fine-tuning** with comprehensive save/export capabilities. It provides:
- **4 main save methods**: LoRA-only, merged 16-bit, merged 4-bit, and TorchAO quantized
- **3 export formats**: Standard HF format, GGUF (for llama.cpp), and GGML
- **16 supported model architectures**: LLaMA, Mistral, Qwen (2, 3, 3-MoE), Gemma, Gemma2, Granite, Cohere, Falcon-H1, and vision models
- **Zero ONNX support** - focuses on open-source deployment formats (GGUF, GGML)
- **Dynamic method patching**: Adds save methods to model instances at runtime
- **Full HuggingFace Hub integration**: Push, tagging, and repository creation
- **Custom CUDA kernels**: Optimized for training speed and memory efficiency

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out
190	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

366	def apply_lora_qkv(self, X, inplace = True):
367	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.q_proj)
368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458

460	def apply_lora_o(self, X):
461	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.o_proj)
462	    OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS = get_lora_parameters(self.o_proj)
463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Report: LoRA Adapter Weights Access and Management in Unsloth

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here is the complete understanding of how LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are accessed and managed:

---

### 1. **LoRA Parameter Extraction Function** (`unsloth/kernels/utils.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**Lines 190-232:** `get_lora_parameters(proj)` function

This is the core function that accesses LoRA weights. It returns a 5-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`.

Key access pattern:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    # Get base layer (either from wrapper or direct)
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Handle disabled adapters
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]  # Extract first adapter from list/tuple
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices via dictionaries
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dictionary access by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]  # Dictionary access by adapter name
    
    # Extract weight tensors
    A = lora_A_linear.weight  # Shape typically (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight  # Shape typically (out_features, r)
    
    # Return with scaling factor
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Lines 235-257:** `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)` function

Similar to above but also returns bias:
```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # Same adapter selection logic
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,  # Also include bias
    )
```

---

### 2. **LoRA Layers Structure** (`unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

**Lines 489-515:** How LoRA layers are accessed in forward pass

```python
# Fast path (single adapter)
if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
    active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
    
    dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
    if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
        # Direct weight access
        lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight  # Linear layer
        lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight  # Linear layer
        scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]       # Float scalar
        W = self.base_layer.weight
        return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)

# Multi-adapter path
for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        continue
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]           # Linear module
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]           # Linear module
    dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]    # Dropout module or Identity
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]         # Float scalar
    
    result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
```

**LoRA Layer Attributes Summary:**
- `self.lora_A` - Dictionary of Linear modules, indexed by adapter name
- `self.lora_B` - Dictionary of Linear modules, indexed by adapter name
- `self.scaling` - Dictionary of float scaling factors (lora_alpha / rank)
- `self.lora_dropout` - Dictionary of Dropout modules or Identity
- `self.active_adapters` - List of currently active adapter names
- `self.disable_adapters` - Boolean flag to disable all adapters
- `self.merged` - Boolean flag indicating if adapters are merged

---

### 3. **LoRA Model Setup** (`unsloth/models/llama.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/models/llama.py`

**Lines 2266-2350:** `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` method

Key parameters and config access:
```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,                                  # Rank
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,                         # Scaling factor
    lora_dropout = 0.0,
    bias = "none",
    layers_to_transform = None,
    layers_pattern = None,
    use_rslora = False,
    modules_to_save = None,
    init_lora_weights = True,
    loftq_config = {},
    **kwargs,
):
    # For already-adapted models, access and validate config
    if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM):
        assert(hasattr(model, "peft_config"))
        
        # Access config dictionary
        peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
        
        # Extract parameters
        old_target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
        modules_to_save = peft_config["modules_to_save"]
        loftq_config = peft_config.get("loftq_config", {})
```

**Lines 2772-2812:** Model PEFT config access after training

```python
# Iterate through all adapters
for active_adapter in model.peft_config.keys():
    name = model.peft_config[active_adapter].base_model_name_or_path
    # ... modifications ...

# Get active adapter
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0] if \
    hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter

# Access LoRA configuration parameters
lora_dropout = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_dropout
bias = model.peft_config[active_adapter].bias
```

**LoRA Config Access Points:**
- `model.peft_config` - Dictionary of LoRA configs, indexed by adapter name
- `model.peft_config["default"]` - Default LoRA config
- `model.peft_config[adapter_name].to_dict()` - Convert config to dictionary
- `model.peft_config[adapter_name].lora_dropout` - Dropout value
- `model.peft_config[adapter_name].bias` - Bias configuration
- `model.peft_config[adapter_name].base_model_name_or_path` - Original model name
- `model.peft_config[adapter_name].loftq_config` - LoFTQ configuration (if used)

---

### 4. **LoRA Config Creation** (`unsloth/models/vision.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/models/vision.py`

**Lines 892-899:** Creating and applying LoRA config

```python
from peft import LoraConfig, TaskType, get_peft_model as _get_peft_model

# Build LoraConfig with signature inspection
local_variables = { **locals(), **kwargs, }
del local_variables["kwargs"]
allowed_parameters = inspect.signature(LoraConfig).parameters.keys()
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    **{ k : v for k, v in local_variables.items() if k in allowed_parameters },
)

# Apply to model
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

**LoraConfig parameters (from PEFT library):**
- `r` - Rank of LoRA adapters
- `lora_alpha` - Scaling factor (alpha/r is the final scaling)
- `target_modules` - List of modules to apply LoRA to
- `lora_dropout` - Dropout rate for LoRA
- `bias` - Bias handling ("none", "all", "lora_only")
- `task_type` - Task type (CAUSAL_LM, SEQ_CLS, etc.)
- `init_lora_weights` - Initialize LoRA weights
- `modules_to_save` - Additional modules to save
- `use_rslora` - Use RSLoRA (Rank-Stabilized LoRA)
- `loftq_config` - LoFTQ configuration dict

---

### 5. **LoRA Weight Merging** (`unsloth/save.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/save.py`

**Lines 159-187:** `_merge_lora(layer, name)` function

This merges LoRA weights into base weights:
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Extract LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        # Dequantize base weight if needed
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        # Merge LoRA: W_merged = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    
    return W, bias
```

**Lines 205-448:** `unsloth_save_model()` function usage context

Called during model saving to handle LoRA merging and weight extraction.

---

### 6. **LoRA Utils and Validation** (`unsloth/models/_utils.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/models/_utils.py`

**Lines 1544-1552:** `validate_loftq_config()` function

```python
def validate_loftq_config(loftq_config, lora_dropout, bias, init_lora_weights, model):
    from peft import LoraConfig
    
    if loftq_config is None: 
        loftq_config = {}
    
    signature = str(inspect.signature(LoraConfig))
    SUPPORTS_LOFTQ = "loftq_config" in signature
    
    if lora_dropout != 0:
        logger.warning_once(
            f"Unsloth: Dropout = 0 is supported for fast patching. "
            f"You are using dropout = {lora_dropout}."
        )
```

---

### **Summary of Access Patterns**

**To access LoRA weights from a trained model:**

```python
# 1. Access adapter configuration
adapter_name = model.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter
config = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
rank = config.r
lora_alpha = config.lora_alpha
scaling_factor = lora_alpha / rank

# 2. Access LoRA weights from PEFT layers
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if hasattr(module, "lora_A") and hasattr(module, "lora_B"):
        lora_A = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight  # Shape: (r, in_features)
        lora_B = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # Shape: (out_features, r)
        scaling = module.scaling[adapter_name]

# 3. Via get_lora_parameters utility
from unsloth.kernels import get_lora_parameters
W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(layer)

# 4. Access merged weights
from unsloth.save import _merge_lora
merged_W, bias = _merge_lora(layer, layer_name)
```

---

### **Key Data Structures**

| Attribute | Type | Content | Indexed By |
|-----------|------|---------|-----------|
| `model.peft_config` | dict | LoRA configurations | adapter name ("default", etc.) |
| `layer.lora_A` | dict | LoRA A matrices (Linear modules) | adapter name |
| `layer.lora_B` | dict | LoRA B matrices (Linear modules) | adapter name |
| `layer.scaling` | dict | Scaling factors (floats) | adapter name |
| `layer.lora_dropout` | dict | Dropout modules | adapter name |
| `model.active_adapters` | list | Currently active adapter names | index (usually 0) |
| `model.active_adapter` | str/tuple | Single active adapter | - |

This comprehensive mapping shows the complete LoRA weight access and management system in unsloth!

### 2. **LoRA Layers Structure** (`unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

**Lines 489-515:** How LoRA layers are accessed in forward pass

```python
# Fast path (single adapter)
if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
    active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
    
    dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
    if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
        # Direct weight access
        lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight  # Linear layer
        lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight  # Linear layer
        scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]       # Float scalar
        W = self.base_layer.weight
        return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)

# Multi-adapter path
for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        continue
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]           # Linear module
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]           # Linear module
    dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]    # Dropout module or Identity
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]         # Float scalar
    
    result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
```

**LoRA Layer Attributes Summary:**
- `self.lora_A` - Dictionary of Linear modules, indexed by adapter name
- `self.lora_B` - Dictionary of Linear modules, indexed by adapter name
- `self.scaling` - Dictionary of float scaling factors (lora_alpha / rank)
- `self.lora_dropout` - Dictionary of Dropout modules or Identity
- `self.active_adapters` - List of currently active adapter names
- `self.disable_adapters` - Boolean flag to disable all adapters
- `self.merged` - Boolean flag indicating if adapters are merged

```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,                                  # Rank
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,                         # Scaling factor
    lora_dropout = 0.0,
    bias = "none",
    layers_to_transform = None,
    layers_pattern = None,
    use_rslora = False,
    modules_to_save = None,
    init_lora_weights = True,
    loftq_config = {},
    **kwargs,
):
    # For already-adapted models, access and validate config
    if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM):
        assert(hasattr(model, "peft_config"))
        
        # Access config dictionary
        peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
        
        # Extract parameters
        old_target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
        modules_to_save = peft_config["modules_to_save"]
        loftq_config = peft_config.get("loftq_config", {})
```

```python
    # ... modifications ...
```

**LoRA Config Access Points:**
- `model.peft_config` - Dictionary of LoRA configs, indexed by adapter name

### 4. **LoRA Config Creation** (`unsloth/models/vision.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/models/vision.py`

**Lines 892-899:** Creating and applying LoRA config

```python
from peft import LoraConfig, TaskType, get_peft_model as _get_peft_model

# Build LoraConfig with signature inspection
local_variables = { **locals(), **kwargs, }
del local_variables["kwargs"]
allowed_parameters = inspect.signature(LoraConfig).parameters.keys()
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    **{ k : v for k, v in local_variables.items() if k in allowed_parameters },
)

# Apply to model
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

**LoraConfig parameters (from PEFT library):**
- `r` - Rank of LoRA adapters
- `lora_alpha` - Scaling factor (alpha/r is the final scaling)
- `target_modules` - List of modules to apply LoRA to
- `lora_dropout` - Dropout rate for LoRA
- `bias` - Bias handling ("none", "all", "lora_only")
- `task_type` - Task type (CAUSAL_LM, SEQ_CLS, etc.)
- `init_lora_weights` - Initialize LoRA weights
- `modules_to_save` - Additional modules to save
- `use_rslora` - Use RSLoRA (Rank-Stabilized LoRA)
- `loftq_config` - LoFTQ configuration dict

### 5. **LoRA Weight Merging** (`unsloth/save.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/save.py`

**Lines 159-187:** `_merge_lora(layer, name)` function

This merges LoRA weights into base weights:
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Extract LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        # Dequantize base weight if needed
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        # Merge LoRA: W_merged = W + scaling * (A^T @ B^T)
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    
    return W, bias
```

**Lines 205-448:** `unsloth_save_model()` function usage context

Called during model saving to handle LoRA merging and weight extraction.

---

### 6. **LoRA Utils and Validation** (`unsloth/models/_utils.py`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505513695-qkokal/unsloth/models/_utils.py`

```python
def validate_loftq_config(loftq_config, lora_dropout, bias, init_lora_weights, model):
    from peft import LoraConfig
    
    if loftq_config is None: 
        loftq_config = {}
    
    signature = str(inspect.signature(LoraConfig))
    SUPPORTS_LOFTQ = "loftq_config" in signature
    
    if lora_dropout != 0:
        logger.warning_once(
            f"Unsloth: Dropout = 0 is supported for fast patching. "
            f"You are using dropout = {lora_dropout}."
        )
```

---

### **Summary of Access Patterns**

**To access LoRA weights from a trained model:**

```python
# 1. Access adapter configuration
adapter_name = model.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter
config = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
rank = config.r
lora_alpha = config.lora_alpha
scaling_factor = lora_alpha / rank

# 2. Access LoRA weights from PEFT layers
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if hasattr(module, "lora_A") and hasattr(module, "lora_B"):
        lora_A = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight  # Shape: (r, in_features)
        lora_B = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # Shape: (out_features, r)
        scaling = module.scaling[adapter_name]

# 3. Via get_lora_parameters utility
from unsloth.kernels import get_lora_parameters
W, quant_state, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(layer)

# 4. Access merged weights
from unsloth.save import _merge_lora
merged_W, bias = _merge_lora(layer, layer_name)
```

---

### **Key Data Structures**

| Attribute | Type | Content | Indexed By |
|-----------|------|---------|-----------|
| `model.peft_config` | dict | LoRA configurations | adapter name ("default", etc.) |
| `layer.lora_A` | dict | LoRA A matrices (Linear modules) | adapter name |
| `layer.lora_B` | dict | LoRA B matrices (Linear modules) | adapter name |
| `layer.scaling` | dict | Scaling factors (floats) | adapter name |
| `layer.lora_dropout` | dict | Dropout modules | adapter name |
| `model.active_adapters` | list | Currently active adapter names | index (usually 0) |
| `model.active_adapter` | str/tuple | Single active adapter | - |

This comprehensive mapping shows the complete LoRA weight access and management system in unsloth!

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass
